title: DIY Tool Organization + Storage
summary: How to build a simple but super functional storage solution for your tools!
description: How to build a simple but super functional storage solution for your tools! This little organization workhorse holds more than you'd ever expect, and keeps your garage organized.

steps.title: Cut Plywood according to cut list
steps.body: Start by cutting down your plywood according to the cut list. Feel free to adjust the sizing depending on what you need for your space.

steps.title: Assemble Side Boxes
steps.body:
Using two 20.25X16 and two 10.5X16 pieces per box, assemble two boxes using pocket holes and wood glue.
I recommend using corner clamps to help you ensure the boxes are square as you assemble.

steps.title: Assemble Drill Hangers
steps.body: Assemble your drill hangers by drilling one 2.25"x8" piece into the bottom of a 4"x8" piece. Be sure to pre-drill 2-3 holes in the smaller piece before drilling.

steps.title: Mark spots on shelf for drill hange
steps.body:
Mark lines on one of the 18"x16" shelves where the drill hangers will go. From center-to-center, the spacing between each drill hanger should be 3.75". The spacing between the side boxes and the first drill hanger on each side should be 3 3/8".
After marking the spots, pre-drill the holes for the drill hangers. You will not hang them until after attaching the shelf.

steps.title: Drill pocket holes in the shelves
steps.body: Drill pocket holes on the short sides of each 18" x 16" shelf.

steps.title: Attach top shelf
steps.body: Attach the top shelf to the two boxes using wood glue and pocket screws. It should be attached 3" from the top of the boxes.

steps.title: Attach second shelf
steps.body: Attach the second shelf in the same manner - it should be 10" from the first shelf.

steps.title: Attach drill hangers to shelf
steps.body: Add wood glue, then the drill hangers into the pre-drilled holes on the bottom shelf.

steps.title: Add shelf pieces to the boxes
steps.body:
Drill pocket holes in the two 9 1/8" x 16" pieces (NOTE: I highly recommend assembling your boxes prior to cutting these pieces, then measuring and cutting based on your box, to account for any variations).
Using wood glue and pocket holes, add a shelf in each box, approximately 13" from the bottom of the shelf (this is obviously an optional step - if you prefer to store taller tools in this space, leave it open).

steps.title: Attach side pieces for drill hanger
steps.body: Using wood glue and screws, attach the two 3/4" x 8" pieces to each box, approximately 2" from the bottom of the box and set back 1/2". (Be sure to pre-drill the holes to avoid splitting the wood)

steps.title: Attach backing
steps.body: Using wood glue and a nail gun, attach the 19" x 21.5" piece to the back of your organizer.

steps.title: Paint and hang
steps.body: Paint in your desired color and hang it up on your garage wall - then, fill it up with all of your favorite tools!
